news_summary: Spain drew Cape Verde 0-0 despite heavy shot volume and are expected to use a 4-3-3 with Yamal, Nico Williams, Oyarzabal, Pedri, Rodri, and Fabian Ruiz. Saudi Arabia drew Uruguay and are expected to defend deeper in a 4-4-2. Saudi backup keeper Nawaf Al Aqidi is the only fitness note; starter Mohammed Al-Owais is unaffected.
robust_model_snapshot: Spain xG 2.131, Saudi Arabia xG 0.814, Spain win 67.27%, draw 19.37%, Saudi win 13.37%, over 2.5 55.91%, most likely 2-0.
model_impact: Spain remain the strongest side in the next four, but the market may be very expensive after Spain's must-win narrative. Without a fresh Asian/total line, avoid deep handicap assumptions.

news_summary: Belgium drew Egypt 1-1 and are expected to start Lukaku with De Bruyne behind him. Debast is out despite returning to individual work, and De Ketelaere is a game-time decision but not projected to start. Iran drew New Zealand 2-2 and remain physical and disciplined.
robust_model_snapshot: Belgium xG 1.676, Iran xG 1.329, Belgium win 45.73%, draw 23.74%, Iran win 30.53%, over 2.5 57.50%, most likely 1-1.
model_impact: Model sees Belgium edge but not dominance. Defensive absences and Iran's structure make Belgium handicap risky; totals need live line confirmation.

news_summary: Uruguay drew Saudi Arabia 1-1 and are without Ronald Araujo and Giorgian De Arrascaeta. Cape Verde held Spain 0-0, have no listed injury-table players, and are expected to stay compact in a 4-5-1.
robust_model_snapshot: Uruguay xG 1.447, Cape Verde xG 1.131, Uruguay win 44.21%, draw 26.04%, Cape Verde win 29.74%, under 2.5 52.88%, most likely 1-1.
model_impact: This is not a clean favorite spot. Uruguay absences and Cape Verde's defensive result against Spain point toward caution on Uruguay handicap; under/draw protection looks more natural if the market line allows.

news_summary: New Zealand drew Iran 2-2 with Elijah Just scoring twice; Egypt drew Belgium 1-1. RotoWire lists no players from either side on the World Cup injury table. Egypt remain favorites through Salah and Marmoush, but New Zealand showed transition and set-piece threat.
robust_model_snapshot: New Zealand xG 1.366, Egypt xG 1.485, New Zealand win 35.15%, draw 24.83%, Egypt win 40.01%, over 2.5 53.88%, most likely 1-1.
model_impact: Model makes this much closer than a reputation-based Egypt favorite. Egypt moneyline/handicap should be price-sensitive; New Zealand protection may be interesting only if line is generous.
